stem: A pupil is choosing whether to store a list of pupils' names with a 1D array or a record (struct) per pupil.
model_answer: A 1D array stores many values of the SAME type — a single column. It is right when every pupil only needs one piece of data (e.g. a name). A record groups several DIFFERENT pieces of data about ONE thing (e.g. name, age, year_group, form_tutor). Records make it easier to keep related fields together and pass them around as a unit, but accessing a value needs a field name (`pupil.name`) rather than just an index. If the pupil only ever needs to store names, an array is simplest; if any extra data per pupil might be added later, a record is the better long-term choice.
review_notes: Teacher-marked. Award marks for: definition of each, contrast (single type vs heterogeneous fields), an applied recommendation.

parts.label: (a)
parts.prompt: Explain the difference between a 1D array and a record, and recommend which the pupil should use to store the data.

mark_points.text: array stores multiple values of the SAME type, accessed by index
record stores several different fields about a single entity, accessed by field name
applied recommendation that fits the scenario (array for names only / record if future fields)
